title: lithocarpus muluensis
intro: Lithocarpus muluensis, also known as the Borneo tanbark oak, is a species of evergreen tree in the beech family native to Borneo. This plant has been found to have various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can be used to treat various health issues such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. The health benefits of Lithocarpus muluensis include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and boost the immune system. Lithocarpus muluensis is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and its extracts can also be used to create herbal capsules or topical creams for medicinal purposes.
